title: Cross-platform reproducibility matrix (OS/arch backends bit-identical)
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. This backlog task hardens the determinism contract by establishing a cross-platform reproducibility matrix (OS/arch x backend) demonstrating bit-identical (P,Q,T) results and recording any platform-specific deviations.
objective: Establish a cross-platform reproducibility matrix demonstrating bit-identical (P,Q,T) results across OS/arch and backends.
acceptanceCriteria: Establishes a cross-platform reproducibility matrix (OS/arch x backend) demonstrating bit-identical (P,Q,T) results.
Documents and hardens the determinism contract; records any platform-specific deviations; CC-BY-4.0.
output: A document presenting the cross-platform reproducibility matrix and determinism-contract hardening.
